definition: The discriminative consciousness associated with a sense field and its object, and central to both aggregate and dependent-origination analysis.
notes: Viññāṇa refers to the consciousness that arises dependent on a sense field and an object. The project keeps consciousness as the default because it is the clearest stable rendering in both aggregate and dependent-origination contexts. Awareness remains acceptable in explanatory prose, but only where it does not imply a permanent substrate or diffuse background self. The term should be heard as conditioned and multiple rather than as a hidden witness behind experience.

context_rules.context: When referring to the six sense consciousnesses
When explanatory prose needs a smoother English line
context_rules.notes: Use carefully and do not imply a permanent substrate.

context_rules.context: When the passage emphasizes the reciprocal relation of consciousness and name-and-form
context_rules.notes: Keep the term firmly within conditioned process rather than metaphysical speculation.

example_phrases.notes: Illustrates one of the six consciousnesses.
A debated phrase; included here as an example only.
